rationale: The execution chain starts with winrshost.exe, leading to cmd.exe, then two layers of PowerShell with encoded commands (A0, EV-001, EV-002). The decoded command (DER-001) shows an attempt to execute a script block and set a variable 'json_raw' from input, which is suspicious. Furthermore, the PowerShell process creates temporary .ps1, .dll, and .cmdline files (EV-003, EV-006, EV-010, EV-011, EV-013) and then executes csc.exe to compile a .NET assembly (EV-012), indicating dynamic code compilation and execution, a common technique for malware.
